academic.title: Academic Scholarship
academic.intro: The academic scholarship is one of the most important merit-based financial grants, awarded to students who have achieved outstanding academic results in their previous active semester.

who.title: Who can receive an academic scholarship?
items: The grant is available to state-funded, full-time students of the University, and up to 50% of students may receive it.
The scholarship is awarded for one academic semester, i.e. a period of five months.

Eligibility and the amount of the scholarship are determined on the basis of the academic results of the most recently completed active semester. A suspended semester does not exclude a student from eligibility; in such cases, the results of the last active semester are taken into account.
It is important to note that first-year students (in Bachelor’s or single-cycle programmes) cannot receive an academic scholarship in their first semester.
If a student is enrolled in parallel programmes, the scholarship may be awarded separately for each programme; however, it may be granted only once for the same academic performance.

calculation.title: How is the scholarship calculated?
paragraphs: The basis of the academic scholarship is the scholarship index, calculated from the performance achieved in the previous active semester.
Depending on the faculty’s regulations, this may be the credit index or the corrected credit index.
Students’ performance is compared within so-called Homogeneous Student Groups (HHCS), which may be formed according to academic year, programme, or specialisation.

decision.title: Who decides on the academic scholarship?
paragraphs: The Faculty Student Union is responsible for the administration of the scholarship. They determine the specific faculty regulations and make the first-instance decision.

The results are published on the Faculty Student Union’s website within five working days following the decision. The published list includes the scholarship indices of each HHCS and the corresponding scholarship amounts.

tjsz.title: Relevant passages from the Student Financial Regulations (TJSZ)
quotes: Up to 50% of full-time, state-funded students at the University may receive an academic scholarship, and the monthly amount awarded to each student must reach at least 5% of the student normative grant.

When awarding the academic scholarship, students must be assigned to Homogeneous Student Groups for a given semester. These groups may be established according to programme, academic year, specialisation, field of study, sub-specialisation, module, subject area, branch, or other curriculum unit, or as a combination of these criteria.

The suspension of the semester(s) preceding the subject semester does not exclude eligibility for the academic scholarship. In such cases, the results of the last active semester must be taken into account.

The Faculty Student Union defines the further principles of faculty-level allocation (including the procedure for evaluating the academic results of students transferred from another higher education institution or faculty, from a different programme or study form, or those who participated in partial studies abroad), as well as the procedural rules and deadlines. These are to be finalised by the beginning of the semester preceding the awarding period and published on the faculty’s website following the approval of the Faculty Study Committee (KTB).
The Faculty Student Union is responsible for coordinating, evaluating, and deciding on the academic scholarship, and for providing official notification of the results.

The results of the academic scholarship are published on the Faculty Student Union’s website within five working days after the decision. The published results must include, for each HHCS, the scholarship index and the corresponding amount of the scholarship.

regular.title: Regular Grant Based on Social Needs
regular.list_title: A student may apply for the Regular Social Scholarship if they,
list_items: are enrolled in a full-time bachelor’s, master’s, single-cycle, or doctoral program, and

are studying in a state-funded or state scholarship-funded form of education, or began their studies in such a form and, based on the number of semesters started in the given program, would still be eligible to participate in a state-funded program.
regular.description_1: Regardless of the number of programs they are enrolled in, each applicant may submit only one application for this scholarship. The application must be submitted through the
regular.description_2: (Unified Application and Evaluation System of the Budapest University of Technology and Economics) platform during the application period.

exceptional.title: Exceptional Grant Based on Social Needs
exceptional.description_1: Students who have become socially disadvantaged due to an unexpected event, or whose social situation has unexpectedly deteriorated, are eligible to apply for the Exceptional Social Scholarship.
exceptional.description_2: The scholarship is a one-time grant. Students may submit multiple applications within a semester if several events justify it, but only one application may be submitted per event. The application must be submitted through the
exceptional.description_3: (Unified Application and Evaluation System of the Budapest University of Technology and Economics) platform.
exceptional.description_4: Before submitting the application, it is recommended to consult with the current president of the University Social Committee regarding the required documents.

social.more_info: For more information, please visit the website of the University Social Committee (ESZB):

items.title: University Student Union Scholarship
paragraphs: Members of the University Student Union are rewarded through this scholarship for carrying out the tasks defined in the Rules and Regulations of the BME Students’ and Doctoral Students’ Union. Reports submitted by the representatives are published on the website of the University Student Union.

items.title: University Doctoral Student Union Scholarship
paragraphs: Members of the University Doctoral Student Union are rewarded through this scholarship for carrying out the tasks defined in the Rules and Regulations of the BME Students’ and Doctoral Students’ Union. Reports submitted by the representatives are published on the website of the University Doctoral Student Union.

items.title: University BME Scholarship
paragraphs: The University BME Scholarship is one of the highest-level recognitions based on professional and scientific achievements that go beyond the requirements defined in the model curricula. Through this scholarship, the University Student Union rewards the most outstanding students who achieved exceptional professional or scientific results during the previous semester.

items.title: National Higher Education Scholarship
paragraphs: For students with outstanding academic results and exceptional professional achievements, the minister responsible for higher education awards the National Higher Education Scholarship for the duration of one academic year. The procedure and details of the evaluation are defined by the Rector’s Instruction issued for the given year.

items.title: University Public Life Scholarship
paragraphs: The University Public Life Scholarship is intended for students who carry out public life activities supporting the work of the University Student Union, such as assisting in the organization of events.

items.title: University Community Scholarship
paragraphs: The University Community Scholarship is intended for students who actively participate in community life at BME. Applications may be submitted for activities carried out in university self-organizing student groups and colleges for advanced studies, in the BME Racing Teams Community, or in the Student International Relations Committee. The aim of the scholarship is to recognize students who create value at the university through their community work alongside their studies.

items.title: Sports Scholarship
paragraphs: Within the framework of the Sports Scholarship, the University Student Union rewards students who, in addition to physical education classes and their university studies, have demonstrated outstanding athletic performance.

items.title: BME “Good Student, Good Athlete” Scholarship
paragraphs: The “Good Student, Good Athlete” Scholarship not only rewards outstanding athletic performance but also takes into account successful academic achievements alongside competitive sports activities. To submit a valid application, the student’s credit index must reach a value of 3.00.

items.title: Scientific Students’ Conference Scholarship
paragraphs: The aim of the scholarship is to recognize outstanding work performed at the Scientific Students’ Conference. Students may apply if they participated in the Scientific Students’ Conference organized at BME and achieved 1st, 2nd or 3rd place, or received a special prize or commendation.

sportterem.title: Gym Application Information
sportterem.description: The **Sport Division of the Budapest University of Technology and Economics (BME)** and the **University Student Representative Council (EHK)** provide an opportunity for sports teams and groups of friends to train in the university's sports facilities at a discounted rate.

facilities.title: Available Sports Facilities
items: **BME Sports Center** (Bertalan Lajos street 4-6, Building ÉL) rooms.
**Dormitory gyms:** Kármán Tódor Dormitory and Bercsényi 28-30 Dormitory gyms.

conditions.title: Conditions for Discounted Rental
conditions.description: Discounted gym rental is available if **80% of the applying team has active full-time student status** at BME in the given semester.

process.title: Application Process and Deadlines
process.description: Applications can be submitted via the specified online form. Completed member lists should be sent via email to **bmesport@umsz.bme.hu** by the deadline specified in the call for applications.
process.warning: **Important!** Incomplete applications or those received after the deadline cannot be accepted.

requiredData.title: Required Data for Application
requiredData.intro: When submitting an application, teams must provide:
items: Team name, Sport
Team leader's name and contact details (responsible for the team within university sports facilities)
Team roster, indicating members with active BME student status
Which gym(s) the team wishes to use
How many times a week the team would train
Which days and time slots are suitable for the team
The team's top 3 results from the last 2 years (if applicable)

selection.title: Evaluation and Priority
selection.intro: The applications are assessed by the Sports Division of BME Operating and Technical Service Ltd., with the approval of the EHK. **In case of oversubscription**, the evaluation committee prioritizes teams based on the following criteria:
items: Teams containing the highest number of **active BME students**.
Teams with **better results** in sports competitions.
selection.warning: **Important!** There is no possibility to appeal the results of the gym scheduling.

usage.title: Information on Rental and Usage
items: **Gym Scheduling:** The final schedule will be available on the date specified in the announcement. All applicants will be notified via email.

**Athletes’ Circle:** Only individuals included on the list submitted during the application process are permitted to play during the assigned time slot. Always carry a photo ID when attending. If an unregistered person is found playing with the team, the team will lose its right to use the facility, and there is no obligation to refund the prepaid rental fee. The roster may be modified slightly during the semester, provided the Sport Division is notified in advance at **bmesport@umsz.bme.hu**.
**Footwear:** Access to the sports courts is permitted only in **non-marking sports shoes with light-colored soles**.
**House Rules:** Compliance with the facility's House Rules is mandatory.

**Dormitory Key Holders:** Teams wishing to use dormitory gyms must designate a maximum of 4 'Key Holders' on their member list. Only these individuals are authorized to pick up keys at the dormitory receptions.
**Kármán/Bercsényi Gyms:** The Kármán gym is primarily intended for training-style bookings. It is recommended to schedule matches or match-oriented training sessions in the Bercsényi gym.

costs.title: Rental Fees (For Information Only)
costs.description: BME students can use the sports facilities at a discount. Rental fees must be paid to the Sport Division.
items: **Dormitory Gyms:** Different hourly rates for peak and off-peak hours.
**BME Sports Center:** Prices may increase in tiers depending on the number of participants.

sportterem.footer: *Accurate information is always contained in the call for applications published for the specific semester.

sportpalyaTamogatas.title: Sports Field Support Application
sportpalyaTamogatas.description: The **Budapest University of Technology and Economics Physical Education Center** and the **University Student Council (EHK)** are announcing a joint application to support the rental of a sports field for **BME student teams**. Our goal is to support regular sports activities among the university's citizens.

application.title: Who can apply?
application.description: Any team that has **at least 80%** of its members in the given semester as full-time students with an active student status at BME can participate in the competition.

period.title: The Period and Location of the Support
items: The support applies to **a semester** (fall or spring semester) of the given academic year.

You can apply not only for sports activities carried out at BME sports facilities (BME Sports Center, Bogdánfy Street Sports Complex, Kármán Tódor Dormitory, Bercsényi 28-30 Dormitory), but also for sports activities held at other external venues.

condition.title: Application Conditions and Submission
condition.intro: An application is valid if the team:
items: Complete and submit the **member list** published as an attachment to the application on time.
Complete and submit **the application form attached** to the application on time.
condition.outro: The application is submitted **electronically**, and the completed attachments must be sent to the email address **info@bmeehk.hu** by the deadline set in the schedule published on the EHK website.

selection.title: Judging Criteria
selection.description: The applications are evaluated by the BME Physical Education Center in cooperation with the University Student Council (EHK). The main criteria are:
items: The team should regularly mobilize **as many people as possible** with the support required.
The team's **achievements in the last 2 years**.
The **proportion of full-time, active BME students** in the team.

result.title: Result and Remedy
items: The EHK will publish the final result **on its website** by the deadline set in the application schedule.
We are unable to accept applications that do not comply with the announcement (in terms of form or content) or that are submitted after the deadline.
In the event of an application with false content, the team may be punished by exclusion and disciplinary proceedings may also be initiated.
Questions about the application and any intention to object to the evaluation can be sent to the e-mail address **info@bmeehk.hu**.

sportpalyaTamogatas.footer: *Exact information is always included in the application announced for the given semester.

info_box.content: The Centre for Modern Languages offers general, professional and examination preparation courses in six foreign languages and at several proficiency levels. Both credit-bearing and non-credit courses are available for students. If you wish to take a language examination, you have the opportunity to do so right here at BME. If you already hold a language certificate but would like to continue developing your language skills, you may also choose from the Centre’s postgraduate specialist training programmes in translation and interpreting, which open up further professional opportunities. payouts.title: Payout Information
payouts.intro_text: The scholarship payout dates for the {title} academic year are as follows:
payouts.important_todo_p1: To ensure successful scholarship payout, you must register your bank account number in Neptun in time; otherwise, the transfer cannot be completed.
payouts.important_todo_p2: Steps to register your bank account: Neptun → Finances → Settings → Bank accounts → Add bank account number.
payouts.important_todo_p3: Additionally, a permanent address registered in Neptun is required for the payout.
payouts.important_todo_p4: If you do not register the necessary data by the third payout attempt, the University is not obliged to initiate another transfer until you notify them that you have corrected the deficiencies.
payouts.wrong_transfer_title: To-Dos in Case of Incorrect Transfer or Termination of Student Status
payouts.wrong_transfer_p1: In the event of an incorrect transfer, you must repay the incorrectly paid amount to the University within 15 days. The same applies upon termination of student status, change of work schedule, or termination of scholarship eligibility; any necessary repayment must be settled within 15 days following the change.
payouts.check_mueper: The specific payout date for the applied scholarship is always contained in the call for applications available on the MŰEPER interface.
payouts.no_active_period: No active payout period at the moment.

general_information.title: General Information
general_information.description: Important General Information for Non-Hungarian Citizens Studying at BME

admission.title: Admission and Study Opportunities
admission.description: BME offers various programs for international students, including:
items: Bachelor’s (BSc), Master’s (MSc), and Doctoral (PhD) programs in English
Exchange programs (mobility programs such as Erasmus+, ATHENS, EELISA)
Both scholarship-based and self-financed (tuition-paying) options.

application.title: Application and Enrollment
items: Applications are submitted online; for example, for the 2025/26 academic year, applications are accepted between April 1 and May 31.
Detailed descriptions of the enrollment process and required documents can be found on the website of the Academic Services Centre (ASC/ETK).

administrative_tasks.title: Administrative Tasks After Arrival
items: Registration at BME is organized by the Academic Services Centre. Here you can find the newsletter on the start of the 2025/26/1 semester: https://www.kth.bme.hu/en/news/2318/
Firstly, you have to upload the necessary documents to Neptun as described in the newsletter. This will be followed by an in-person registration, where your documents will be checked.
Subjects can be registered in Neptun at Subjects/Register for subjects after you registered for the term as an active student.

official_documents.title: Official documents you will need
items: **Residence permit**: To stay and study in Hungary you are required to obtain a residence permit. It can take quite a long time so you should start as early as possible. You can find more information here: https://www.imt.bme.hu/immigration/application/
**TAJ Card/Private Health Insurance:** Applying for a TAJ card (Hungarian health insurance card) and a tax card. More information: https://www.imt.bme.hu/taj/
**Student ID Card:** Find out more here: https://www.kth.bme.hu/en/for-students/faq/student-id-card/
Opening a **Hungarian bank account** (for scholarships, tuition, and other transfers)

academic_affairs.title: Academic Affairs and Support
items: Academic administration is managed by the Academic Services Centre and the faculty study offices.

The university ensures that students have access to all important regulations and information in the language of instruction (e.g., academic calendar, scholarships, disciplinary rules, appeals, healthcare).
Dedicated support: international mentor teams, advisors, and faculty Erasmus coordinators are available.

tasks_by_sept_1.title: Tasks to Complete by September 1st
items: Get familiar with the Neptun system
Start using your @edu.bme.hu email address
Install Office applications (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, etc.)
Register your Office 365 account using your university email address to access: OneDrive storage, Microsoft Teams, University Wi-Fi
Other related services
Learn to use the educational platforms (e.g., Moodle, Teams, or other systems used in your faculty)

neptun.title: Neptun System
neptun.description: Neptun is the system used at the university for managing all academic and study-related matters.
items: Enroll for the semester
Register for subjects
View your personal timetable
Sign up for exams
neptun.footer: With your admission letter, you received a 6-character unique identifier (your Neptun code) and an initial password. Neptun guides: https://kth.bme.hu/en/for-students/about-neptun/neptun-guides/

eduid.title: BME Directory (eduID)
paragraphs: At BME, most university websites use the **BME Directory (eduID)** for login (Moodle, KEFIR, MŰEPER, etc.).
Every student is automatically assigned a **directory ID**, which can be found on the directory administration page, after logging in with your Neptun login credentials.
Once logged in, you’ll see your **directory ID** next to the label **eduID**.
During your first login, you must change the initial password, and later you can also use this site to update your password anytime.
From that point on, when a university website prompts you with the eduID login page, => use your **directory ID** and the **password you set**.
On the same admin page, you can also request access to Office 365 (O365), which gives you access to: University Wi-Fi, Microsoft Teams, OneDrive and other O365 services.

o365.title: O365 Registration (Microsoft 365)
o365.description: Your O365 account gives you access to the following services: Microsoft Teams, Your @edu.bme.hu email address, 1 TB of OneDrive cloud storage, The BME Wi-Fi network (and Eduroam networks at universities worldwide). *(Microsoft has renamed O365 to M365, most university pages still refer to it as O365.)*
steps: Log in to the BME Directory Administration Page using your Neptun login details.
Select the menu: "Set up user account for Microsoft Office 365"
Choose your O365 username (you can set this yourself).
o365.footer: Your access will be activated within 24 hours.

moodle.title: MOODLE
moodle.list_title: You can find courses in two ways:
items: By browsing based on language, department, and course name, or
From the left-hand navigation menu, where only your own registered courses are listed — making them quick and easy to access.

teams.title: Using MS Teams
paragraphs: **Microsoft Teams** is an online communication platform that supports **video calls** and **virtual meetings**.
At the university, instructors currently use it mainly for **online classes** and **consultations**.
You can access Teams via: Web browser, Mobile app, Desktop app.
We recommend using the desktop application on a laptop or computer for the best experience. To log in, use your **O365 account** (your **@edu.bme.hu email address**).

wifi.title: Using the University Wi-Fi Network
wifi.description: The easiest way to connect to the university **Wi-Fi network** is by using your **O365 account**, so if you haven’t registered yet, it’s worth doing it first. A detailed **setup guide** is available here: https://net.bme.hu/wlan/eduroam-bme.php?lang=en

office.title: Installing Office Applications
office.description: At https://www.office.com/, after logging in with your **eduID**, you’ll see an option in the top-right corner to install **Office apps** on your device.
items: Word
Excel
PowerPoint
OneDrive
Outlook
Teams
OneNote
office.footer: Free Software Available for Students: https://net.bme.hu/sw/

ehk.title: Students’ Union (EHK)
paragraphs: The Students’ Union at the Budapest University of Technology and Economics (BME) is the official **student representative** organization. Its main role is to advocate for students’ interests in university decision-making processes and in everyday matters. Members of the Students’ Union are elected by the students, and they assist with academic, social, dormitory, and other student-related issues.
The Students’ Union operates at each faculty and also has a university-level organization, which coordinates the work of the faculty-level student councils.
You can contact us if you have any problems either with your studies, dormitory, scholarships or social assistance on everything explained above.
items: Acts as a bridge between students and the university administration.
Informs students about important news, opportunities, and regulations affecting them.
Participates in the preparation and review of university policies and decisions.
Organizes community, sports, and cultural events, and supports student life.

student_life.organizations_intro: At the Budapest University of Technology and Economics (BME), numerous professional colleges (szakkollégium) and student-led organizations (öntevékeny kör) operate, offering outstanding opportunities for students’ professional, academic, community, and personal development.

mvk.title: BME Competitive Team Community (MVK)
mvk.description: The Community of Competitive Teams at the Budapest University of Technology and Economics (MVK) was established with the aim of supporting the student teams that build competition vehicles at the university in a coordinated manner. It also seeks to provide its members with opportunities for professional development that complement their academic education. Another key goal of MVK is to promote and represent the university at national and international events.

professional_colleges.title: Professional Colleges (Szakkollégiumok)
professional_colleges.description: Professional colleges are student communities that provide programs closely related to, but going beyond, regular university studies. They offer professional, academic, research, and community activities. Members can work together in an active and inspiring environment, participate in specialized trainings, courses, workshops, lectures, and team-building programs.
items: Simonyi Károly Professional College
BME Architecture Club Professional College
BME Mechanical Engineering Professional College
BME Transportation Engineering Professional College
BME Management Professional College
BME Zielinski Szilárd Civil Engineering Professional College
University Cosmos Professional College
and many more…

student_led.title: Student-led Organizations (Öntevékeny körök)
student_led.description: Student-led organizations are university or faculty groups founded by students according to their interests, and they engage in professional, academic, sports, cultural, or community-building activities. These groups provide opportunities for students to try themselves in various fields, such as IT projects, photography, economics, or sports. Student-led organizations actively contribute to the community life of the faculties and the university, organize programs, and help students integrate and develop.
items: Hungarian Folk Dance Club
Diving Club
Fencing Committee
Symphony Orchestra
and many more…

culture.title: Culture on Campus
paragraphs: **Szkéné Theatre** is the university's independent theatre **located on the campus of BME**. It has been a unique cultural hub for students and the wider public since 1970. Known for its contemporary, experimental performances and collaborations with renowned directors and theatre companies, Szkéné offers a diverse repertoire ranging from drama and physical theatre to modern interpretations of classical works. It provides students not only with high-quality cultural experiences but also opportunities to engage with the performing arts in a thought-provoking and inspiring environment.

For buses 100E and 200E, a special airport ticket is required (which can be bought on the buses, at the airport, or in the BudapestGO app).
Download the BudapestGO app to conveniently buy a pass.

where_available.title: Where it’s available:
where_available.description: Every subject offered by the University must have a subject description. This must be available in the SAS system or on the faculty’s website.

languages.title: Languages:
languages.description: Subject descriptions must be provided in:
items: Hungarian
English
The language of the training programme (if different)

approval.title: Approval:
items: For some subjects (as defined in Section 17. (3) a & b), the Faculty Council must approve the subject description.
For others (as defined in Section 17. (3) c), a designated person or body (as decided by the Faculty Council) approves it.

must_include.title: What the Subject Description Must Include:
items: Subject title
Subject code (from Section 19)
Language(s) it's offered in
Type of subject (based on Section 17. (2))
Role in the curriculum for each academic programme (Section 17. (3))
Course type and number of hours per week or per term
Number of credits
Type of evaluation (based on Section 18)
Name, contact info, and department of the subject coordinator
Department responsible for teaching
Purpose of the subject and how it connects to the programme's learning goals
Requirements to register (see Section 37)
Learning outcomes, aligned with the programme’s training goals
How learning outcomes are evaluated (see Title 31)
Learning materials (print or digital) and where to access them

registration_periods.title: Course Registration Periods at BME
paragraphs: Unless stated otherwise, the preliminary subject and course registration period starts at 18:00 on the third working day after the exam period and ends at 12:00 on the last working day before the registration week.

For exact dates and times, students should always check the official ASC website. Also you can always find all the dates on the websites, Instagram and Facebook of the Students’ Union or Faculty Student Councils.
The final course registration period starts at 18:00 on the first day of the registration week and runs until 23:59 on the last day of that week.

late_registration.title: Late Subject Registration or Withdrawal at BME
paragraphs: It’s possible to register for or drop a subject after the regular period, but only until the first day of the second teaching week. You must submit a request via Neptun:
requests: Request 018 – Subject registration/withdrawal request (NOT your own fault)
Request 019 – Late subject registration/withdrawal request (YOUR own fault) - A 5,500 HUF fee per subject applies if it’s your own fault.

not_own_fault.title: What counts as "not your own fault"?
not_own_fault.intro: You can use the 018 form without a fee if, for example:
items: You were entitled to register the subject according to your curriculum, but Neptun didn’t allow it or deleted it by mistake.
You completed the subject in a previous programme, but want to register it again (not just get it recognized).
The course time changed and now conflicts with another subject, so you need to drop it.
You couldn’t register for a specialization course due to late specialization approval.
You want to register for a course from another faculty.
Other justified reasons.
not_own_fault.outro: You should attach instructor approval using the official form. This is strongly recommended in all cases. It becomes mandatory starting from the third teaching week.

study_levels.title: There are a lot of different types of study levels at BME
items: bachelor training programme (BA, BSc);
master training programme (MA, MSc);
single-cycle training programme;
doctoral training programme (PhD, DLA, DBA).
study_levels.extra: Also the University organises postgraduate specialist training programmes, which do not award a higher education degree.

languages.title: Following their statutory registration, by the Office of Education, the academic programmes may be announced in the following languages:
items: Hungarian
English
German

formats.title: Programmes under Section 11. may be organised in
items: full-time (daytime),
part-time (evening or correspondence), or
distance learning format.

subjects.title: Subjects based on their role in the curriculum, a subject can be
items: mandatory;
elective whose adding and completion is mandatory, and of which students have to add and complete a given number of credits as specified in the curriculum;
optional, not identified by the curriculum on an itemised basis, but of which students have to add and complete a given number of credits; or
a criterion requirement of which students have to add and complete a given number of subjects to obtain their degree or qualification. Such as PE class, etc…

problems.title: If problems occur during your studies…

professor.title: you should contact your professor if…
items: …you have problems with your homework
…you don't understand something
…you couldn't attend a laboratory class, and you have to go to another
etc.

students_council.title: You should contact the Students’ Council of your Faculty if
items: …the whole class have the same problem,
…over 50% fail on a test and exam,
…if you don’t understand something about the CoS

evaluation.title: Different methods of evaluation and assessment of learning outcomes

assessment_goals.title: Learning Outcome Assessment
assessment_goals.intro: Assessing learning outcomes helps achieve the following goals:
items: Diagnostic Assessment: To identify what students already know before starting an activity.
Formative Assessment: To monitor students' progress during the course, with ongoing feedback from the instructor.
Practical Work: To guide students as they work on projects, presentations, creative work, or other tasks with instructor support.
Dual/Cooperative Training: To solve practical tasks in training that involves both school and workplace settings.

diagnostic.title: Diagnostic Assessment (before starting an activity):
items: Checks if students have the basic knowledge/skills needed.
Can be graded as “satisfactory”/“fail” or with points.
Grades may or may not count toward the final grade.

formative.title: Formative Assessment (during the course):
items: Based on class participation, group work, oral reports, lab work, homework, etc.
Helps track progress and gives students feedback to improve.
If required for a subject, it must count for at least 15% of the final grade (except for pass/fail requirements).

summative.title: Summative Assessment (at the end of a course or topic):
items: Assesses what students have learned overall.
Examples: exams (written, oral, practical), complex assessments.
May include one or more types of tests.

ohv.title: Evaluation of Teachers by Students (OHV):
ohv.intro: At Budapest University of Technology and Economics (BME), student feedback is an important part of improving education quality. The university makes sure students have the chance to share their opinions about courses and teaching.

why_matters.title: Why student feedback matters:
items: If a teacher agrees in writing, students can see what others thought of the course.
Teachers get useful feedback to improve their teaching.
Faculties and the university learn how students view their programmes.
Overall, feedback helps BME improve the quality of its education.
why_matters.extra: (Also as a reward for taking part in the OHV you can take up courses from 16:00 instead of 18:00.)

absences.title: Absences
paragraphs: The CoS regulates how many lessons teachers can require you to attend during the semester to complete the course.

For lectures, there is no minimum attendance requirement set by the CoS, but teachers can require you to attend, at most 70% of the lectures during a semester, to pass if the subject description includes.

how_it_works.title: How a semester works:
how_it_works.intro: Each semester (both fall and spring) is divided into 5 parts, each of which is important and complicated in its own regard, here we will only talk about the most important things to know about each.

stages.title: Registration week (3-5 workdays)
stages.description: This is when you need to set your semester to active, and decide which classes you want to take. (or earlier, see above). It's important to check, whether or not you have any payments left to cover to the university, as any payment that has passed its deadline, will prevent you from registering to any classes.

stages.title: Active Semester period (At least 14 weeks, but at most 70 workdays)
stages.description: This is the main part of the semester, students attend classes, midterm tests are written, assignments need to be made, etc. The exam registration is also scheduled for the 14th week.

stages.title: Retake week (5 days)
stages.description: Most test retakes and late assignment submissions happen this week, so for some, this may be a very difficult and long week, while some may not have anything left to do. Be careful to not end up with more than you can handle this week. Also there might be a possibility to take an early exam, this depends on the subject.

stages.title: Exam period (20 workdays)
stages.description: These 4 weeks are where all your exams will take place. Much like how at the beginning of the semester, when you need to register for classes, before the exam period, you will need to register for exams, which is also prevented if you have any late payments left to the university. Exams can be retaken as many times as possible, if there are exam times, and student spaces on them left in the semester, however, from the second retake of an exam (3rd time going), it will cost a fee, which you can pay later. There are also 10 extra workdays left after the exam period for final exams.

calendar.title: Mid-term test and assignment calendar
paragraphs: Test and assignment calendars are important tools to keep organized during the semester. Since every faculty has specific needs, they are usually made at a faculty, or Student Council level. Ask around if your faculty, or Student Council makes test calendars, and if so, where you can find them.
If not, you can make your own, to keep track of the tests, assignments and their deadlines, to not get lost during the semester.

completion.title: Completion of Studies
paragraphs: You can find the general rules of completing your studies in Chapter V. of the Code of Studies.
The exact way of completing your studies varies based on the faculty you study at, and the course you're taking, you can find out these specifics at your faculty's website

curriculum.title: Curriculum and prerequisite system
paragraphs: Whichever faculty you take your classes at, everyone will have a curriculum and prerequisite system, where you can see, which subjects you need to complete, to take the next one. These can be usually found on the faculty website, and are an extremely important tool, to know how long you have left of the university, especially if you fail some classes

tdk.title: Students' Scientific Conference (TDK)
tdk.intro: TDK (Students' Scientific Association) is a chance to dive into a topic you love, do real research, and present your findings at a university conference. You’ll work with a lecturer (your advisor) and write a paper, then present it in November at BME’s TDK Conference.

why.title: Why participate in TDK?
items: Explore a topic you're passionate about
Gain research and presentation experience
Boost your CV and scholarship/PhD chances
Use your TDK paper as a base for your thesis
Extra points for Erasmus+, Master’s programs, and more

who.title: Who can apply?
items: University students with an advisor and a specific topic
High school students interested in research

how.title: How to get started:
items: Pick a topic that fascinates you
Find a lecturer to be your advisor
Start working in spring or early summer

timeline.title: Timeline:
items: September: Apply with your topic, advisor, and summary
November: Submit your paper + give your presentation
The best projects move on to the national OTDK round
timeline.extra: Check your faculty’s TDK page for deadlines and topic ideas.

application_information.title: Scholarship Opportunities & Neptun Requests
application_information.intro: The Budapest University of Technology and Economics (BME) offers a wide range of scholarship opportunities to support students' academic performance and personal development. Scholarships are available both at the university level and at the faculty level, ensuring that students from all fields of study can benefit from tailored support.

items.title: University BME scholarship (Egyetemi BME Ösztöndíj)
items.description: The aim of the competition is to reward those among the university students who have achieved the most professional and scientific merit in the semester specified in the competition announcement. The competition is open to active full-time students studying in full-time undergraduate, master's or undivided programs who have engaged in outstanding academic, professional and scientific activities.

items.title: Good Student, Good Athlete (Jó Tanuló, Jó Sportoló)
items.description: The BME "Good Student, Good Athlete" scholarship can be awarded to an applicant whose scholarship index in the last two semesters spent at the University is at least 3.00, or if he/she has achieved results in one of the sports categories listed below: Olympics and World Championships; European Championships, World Cup, UNIVERSIADE; Age-group World and European Championships; Hungarian Championships, international competitions; MEFOB, age-group national championships; National and regional competitions, Universitas; Other competitions.

faculty_scholarships.title: Faculty-level scholarships

items.title: Közéleti ösztöndíj (Public Life Scholarship)
items.description: The respective Student Unions support students with this scholarship who, in addition to their university studies, carry out community activities at the given BME faculty that contribute to the successful work of the Faculty Student Union.
items.faculties: ÉMK, GPK, VBK, VIK, ÉPK, GTK

items.title: Közösségi ösztöndíj (Community Scholarship)
items.description: The respective Student Unions support students with this scholarship who, beyond fulfilling their academic obligations, have performed outstanding community work within the Faculty's Student Organizations, external committees, or as organizers of events.
items.faculties: GPK, VBK, ÉPK, GTK, KJK, TTK

items.title: Hallgatók az oktatásért ösztöndíj (Students for Education Scholarship)
items.description: The respective Student Unions support students with this scholarship who, alongside their university studies, have carried out tutoring activities at the given BME faculty during the semester specified in the call for applications.

items.title: Kari BME ösztöndíj (Faculty BME Scholarship)
items.description: The aim of the application is to provide special recognition to those students within the given faculty who achieve the most professional and scientific merits during the academic year specified in the call for applications. The scholarship rewards students who have performed outstanding professional and scientific activities at the faculty level, including exceptional academic or language achievements.
items.faculties: VBK, GPK, ÉPK, VIK, KJK, GTK

items.title: Sportösztöndíj (Sports Scholarship)
items.description: The respective Student Unions announce a scholarship application to recognize and encourage students who, in addition to fulfilling their academic obligations, have achieved outstanding results in sports.

faculty_scholarships.note: The application requirements may differ by faculty, so it is important to consult the website or contact the respective Faculty Student Council via email to get accurate information about the activities eligible for application.

submission.title: Submission process
submission.description: Applications for the scholarships must be submitted via the MŰEPER (Unified Application and Evaluation System of the Budapest University of Technology and Economics) platform. This may vary by faculty, so please consult the appropriate contact information for details. You can also receive assistance during the submission process.

neptun_requests.title: Some of the most important requests in Neptun
neptun_requests.note: There might be requests not shown here, if it is for/by the Faculties or the Directorate of Sales and Services.

items.title: Appeal
items.description: This is a request for reconsideration of the decision on another request.

items.title: Request for equity (Faculty Chance)
items.description: This request is for asking exemption from any one of the BME Code of Studies regulations. You can get faculty chance only once during your studies!

items.title: Request for tuition fee reduction
items.description: This request is for tuition fee paying students, who can ask for tuition fee reduction.

items.title: Request to register for subjects / to drop subjects
items.description: It is available each term and until the end of the registration week it is free, after that you have to pay 5500 HUF/ course through your Neptun account. For more details, please read ASC instructions among Neptun guides.

items.title: Request for Preferential Course Schedule
items.description: To receive permission to arrive late and follow the classes in asynchronous mode by professors' teaching material.

items.title: Request for subject accreditation
items.description:
By this request, you can transfer credits from your previous studies. For more details, please read ASC instruction among Neptun guides.
1. Pre-BME-studies accreditation: it is basically part of the admission process, especially for transfer students. In this case, you have to fill out the Excel file for request E024 with the Faculty Credit Transfer Committee, and MUST include the decision columns.
2. Parallel-BME-studies accreditation: if you took part in a summer school, exchange program, etc., parallel to your BME studies, you may accredit the subjects/marks you obtained outside of BME. In this case, please DO NOT fill out the decision columns of the table, the Credit Transfer Committee will do so!

items.title: Request for a certificate
items.description: Through this request you can ask for any kind of official certificate/document, e.g.: General Student Status Certificate, Transcript, Diploma copy etc. For some documents you will need to pay, but it will be shown in the request, please read it carefully when submitting it! The digital documents can be downloaded from the request supplement.

items.title: Request for changing institution, major or/and language of studies for SH/HDS/SCYP students
items.description: The request will be available in the Spring term between mid March – mid April, in the Fall term between mid October – mid November.

items.title: Resignation from enrolment opportunity
items.description: This request is for every new student at the beginning of studies in order to resign from the enrolment opportunity. In relation with the student's enrolment for the concerned training programme especially with the student's registration for the concerned term is void according to the request and the student's term can be cancelled.

items.title: Presentation of document
items.description: Study contract, Declaration of state scholarship, Certificate of language exam, Learning agreement of incoming Erasmus+ mobility students, SCYP Travel allowance declaration, Credit certificate for checking the minimum credit requirement, Authorisation, Certificate for data in diploma and declaration of consent for transmission.

items.title: Online registration
items.description: This request is for every new student at the beginning of studies in order to complete the enrolment.

items.title: Request to pay the tuition fee in installments and/or to modify the deadline of tuition fee payment
items.description: It can be submitted from an inflicted item.

items.title: Request for late activation of the term
items.description: This request is available after the registration period for 1 week. It is necessary to have 5500 HUF on your Neptun bank account before submitting the request.

items.title: Request for International Bank Account registration
Request for a purpose not specified in any other requests

eligibility.title: Who is eligible to apply for a dormitory place?
eligibility.description: Any student who establishes a student status with the Budapest University of Technology and Economics (BME) and registers for an active semester is eligible to apply for a dormitory place.
eligibility.dormitory_types_title: Dormitories you can apply to are the following:

dormitory_types.name: Óbudai Diák Hotel
dormitory_types.description: for Stipendium Hungaricum and Diaspora scholarship holders

dormitory_types.name: Martos Kollégium
dormitory_types.description: for SCYP (Scholarship for Christian Young People) holders

dormitory_types.name: BME dormitories
dormitory_types.description: fee-paying students can apply for accommodation in 7 dormitories, each assigned to a different faculty.

frequency.title: How often do students need to apply for accommodation?
frequency.description: Dormitory places must be applied for each semester individually, meaning that a granted place is valid only for one semester. Applications are organized separately for the autumn and spring semesters, as well as for the summer period (July–August).

admission.title: How can students apply for a dormitory place, and how is admission determined?
admission.description: Dormitory places are allocated based on a competitive application process.

ranking.detail: establishing their first student status with the Budapest University of Technology and Economics (BME), the ranking for admission is based on both academic performance (admission score) and social circumstances.
the ranking is based on academic performance, social circumstances and community activities carried out for the benefit of university life.

application_steps.title: Steps to Apply for Dormitory Accommodation in the Autumn Semester

groups.title: Stipendium Hungaricum and Diaspora students:
groups.intro: If you intend to live in a dormitory during the autumn semester, you must complete the following steps:
steps: Submit your application through the Unified Dormitory Admission and Information System (KEFIR) at http://kefir.bme.hu/. You can log in using your Directory ID, which you can create with your Neptun code.

Complete your profile on the KEFIR platform, then open the appropriate dormitory application form for the current (for example 2025/2026) autumn semester, and carefully fill in the required fields with the necessary information. Be mindful of the application deadline listed on the website.

Applications can only be submitted during the specified period; late submissions will not be accepted. Only students who have successfully submitted their application will be considered for a dormitory place.
By submitting the dormitory application, you officially express your request for accommodation.
Students who are recipients of the Stipendium Hungaricum or Diaspora Scholarship will be accommodated in the Óbuda Diák Hotel.

groups.title: Fee-paying students:
groups.intro: If you intend to live in a dormitory during the autumn semester, you must complete the following steps:
steps: Submit your application through the Unified Dormitory Admission and Information System (KEFIR) at http://kefir.bme.hu/. You can log in using your Directory ID, which you can create with your Neptun code.

Complete your profile on the KEFIR platform, then open the appropriate dormitory application form for the current (for example 2025/2026) autumn semester, and carefully fill in the required fields with the necessary information. Be mindful of the application deadline listed on the website.

Applications can only be submitted during the specified period; late submissions will not be accepted. Only students who have successfully submitted their application will be considered for a dormitory place.
By submitting the dormitory application, you officially express your request for accommodation.
Applications will be evaluated by the Dormitory Representative of the Students' Union.

mobility_programs.title: Mobility Opportunities
mobility_programs.intro: For fee-paying international students enrolled at the Budapest University of Technology and Economics (BME), several mobility opportunities are available, offering both short-term and long-term academic or professional experiences abroad.

programs.title: Erasmus+ Traineeship
programs.description: BME students may complete a professional internship abroad through the Erasmus+ Traineeship programme. This option is available for bachelor's, master's, and doctoral students, either during their studies or within one year after graduation. Internships can take place at companies, research centers, or other relevant institutions in Erasmus+ programme countries. The duration ranges from 2 to 12 months, and participants receive a monthly grant to support their living expenses. Students must be actively enrolled at BME at the time of application.
programs.extra: Stipendium Hungaricum Scholarship students are also eligible to apply for Erasmus+ traineeships. They can apply only after graduation.

programs.title: EELISA
programs.description: BME is a core member of the European Engineering Learning Innovation and Science Alliance (EELISA), a European University alliance that aims to redefine engineering education by fostering innovation, sustainability, and societal engagement. Through EELISA mobility actions, students can participate in short-term study visits, joint learning activities, research projects, or challenge-based initiatives at partner institutions across Europe. These activities are often multidisciplinary, may award ECTS credits, and are open to bachelor's, master's, and doctoral students. EELISA also promotes micro-credentials and the recognition of skills acquired during these engagements.
programs.extra: You can find out about current opportunities on the following page:

programs.title: ATHENS
programs.description: The ATHENS (Advanced Technology Higher Education Network SOCRATES) programme is a short-term exchange initiative coordinated by a network of leading European technical universities, including BME. It offers students the opportunity to attend intensive, one-week interdisciplinary courses abroad, typically in March or November. Courses cover a wide range of scientific, technical, and humanities subjects, and are combined with cultural activities. Participants may receive academic credit upon successful completion, and the programme is particularly suitable for students looking to enhance their international exposure in a compact format.

programs.title: CEEPUS Programme
programs.description: Through the CEEPUS (Central European Exchange Program for University Studies), BME students can study for a semester or a shorter period at universities in Central and Eastern European countries participating in the program. The CEEPUS scholarship supports living expenses, and the program is open to bachelor's, master's, and doctoral students.

subsections.title: Bilateral Exchange Programs
subsections.description: BME has established numerous bilateral agreements with universities worldwide, allowing students to study for one or two semesters at partner institutions, usually without paying tuition fees. Application to these programs requires nomination from the sending institution as well as online registration at BME.

subsections.title: Semester Abroad Program
subsections.description: BME also offers its own Semester Abroad program, in which students can spend a semester at the university and take English-language courses from various faculties. Successfully completed courses are awarded ECTS credits, which can be transferred to the student's home university.

programs.title: AIESEC Internships
programs.description: AIESEC, a global student organization present at BME, offers international internship opportunities for students seeking practical experience in various fields such as marketing, IT, engineering, or education. These internships are typically 6 weeks to several months long, and are available in numerous countries worldwide. AIESEC provides support with placement, visa, and accommodation. These internships are not always credit-bearing, but they offer valuable real-world experience and cultural exchange.

contact.title: CONTACT
contact.university: Budapest University of Technology and Economics

korok.title: BME Aerospace Team
description: The BME Aerospace Team is an innovative student rocketry team with a committed and motivated membership, where technical developments are coupled with a high emphasis on student knowledge expansion and community building.

Research rockets are single- or multi-stage vehicles that typically transport scientific experiments into the atmosphere using solid-propellant rocket motors. The BME Aerospace Team was created to build research rockets and pave the way for those interested in space research and space technology, particularly the world of rockets.

The BME Aerospace Team's developments focus on onboard computers, ground equipment (e.g., antenna systems), and active aerodynamic control. The team launches its experiments using its own developed solid-propellant or hybrid-powered rockets.
The BME Aerospace Team eagerly awaits students interested in space sciences!

korok.title: Squash Club
description: The BME Squash Club started in 2012 with the aim of bringing together university students who love squash and helping them improve in the sport. The club was on hiatus for the past few years, but we revived it in 2023, and it is now more active than ever.

Our current goal is to introduce squash to as many university students as possible and help them make active, regular exercise a part of their daily lives. To this end, we hold hobby and competitive training sessions. The essence of the experience-based division is to provide the most high-quality training for everyone; therefore, we strive to have players of similar levels train together. However, transitioning between levels is informal, so we handle this flexibly, and the system is also influenced by who can join us on which day. We want to give everyone a chance to try this sport, so anyone can come for even a single training session throughout the year, and if they like it, they can join us for exercise multiple times a week. Our training sessions are extremely complex; we introduce students to all aspects of squash, develop their hitting technique, stamina, and concentration skills; thus, the training moves and recharges the entire body and soul of the participants with energy. Our coaches are former junior competitors who provide the best of their 10+ years of knowledge and experience to students so they can get the maximum out of themselves.

In addition to training, we organize house championships and team-building sessions where our players can test their skills, experience their progress, and everyone can get to know each other better in the process.
We recommend this sport to those who want to relieve daily stress alongside studying, move their bodies, or just want to be members of a cohesive, good-spirited team.

korok.title: University Green Club
description: The EZK is an active, creative community operating in the spirit of environmental awareness and sustainability at the university and its surroundings.

Our history dates back to the 1980s, when it was first established at BME. Initially, we drew attention to the importance of environmental protection by organizing leaflet distributions and trash clean-ups. The organization was on hiatus in the '90s but was revived in 2005, and since then, our scope of activities and membership have been continuously expanding.

The goal of EZK is to make environmental awareness part of our daily lives. Our activities include various eco-friendly projects, such as building green walls from waste materials, creating raised beds and flower boxes from discarded pallets, and establishing bird-friendly gardens on the university campus, where students and faculty can enjoy the green environment.
With our projects, we make not only the campus but also the dormitories and surrounding districts more beautiful and livable.

In addition to our awareness-raising work, we organize numerous leisure programs, clothing swaps, lectures, and workshops, through which students can acquire practical knowledge in the field of environmental protection. We regularly participate in trash clean-up actions, such as on the Danube bank and Gellért Hill, directly contributing to preserving the cleanliness of our environment. We also organize trash-collecting canoeing, where we collect trash floating on the Danube.
If you love creative activities, are interested in environmental protection, and want to be part of a dynamic team, don't hesitate! Get in touch with us and join the BME University Green Club!
Together we can work to make our university and environment greener and more sustainable.
Don't miss out, every hand is needed for the success of environmental protection!

korok.title: BME Lacrosse
description: Hi everyone!

Our student club was created for lovers of extreme sports in 2020. Lacrosse as a sport is easy to describe: many strong people fight with sticks for a small ball, with which they then try to score a goal in the opponent's goal. Joking aside, the men's version of the sport is highly contact-oriented, while the women's is much more based on fast ball play. It is difficult to introduce the sport because, as things stand, it is played in three different rule systems. The large-scale traditional version is called field, the more aggressive version is box, and the newest one, which will return to the Olympics in 2028, is called sixes.

The field version of the sport is played on a standard-sized football pitch with 9+1 players per team; its specialty is that the goals are not at the end of the pitch but are placed about 10 meters further in so that play can also happen behind the goal.
The game is more physical; the other version, called box lacrosse, is played in hockey arenas; here the rules are a bit looser, but we wear significantly more protective gear to avoid injuries.
The newest version is played with a 5+1 lineup (also per team) on a significantly smaller grass pitch.

Our members were persuaded by their love for the sport and our team's strong community to try to convey the magnificence of our sport to as many students as possible. If you would like to join our student club, then search for us at the links below. Currently, we have also started to open towards ice hockey, so if that interests you, feel free to contact us at our contact details below:

korok.title: Műegyetemi Néptáncegyüttes
description: The Műegyetemi Néptáncegyüttes (MENTE) started in 2001 from the Faculty of Architecture, and with two decades of continuous expansion, it has grown into one of the university's largest student cultural clubs and a well-known player in the Budapest dance house scene. The goal of the ensemble is for university youth to get to know and master the dances of the Carpathian Basin and maintain the traditions of Hungarian peasant culture. MENTE warmly welcomes all those interested to its beginner, intermediate, and advanced groups. If you want to be a member of a super, cohesive community, all you have to do is come to our first rehearsals at the beginning of the semester. For further information or in case of questions, feel free to contact us at the following contacts: neptanc.bme.hu, neptanc.bme@gmail.com, facebook.com/neptanc.bme

korok.title: BME Diving Club
description: The BME Diving Club looks back on a decade of history. It was founded by Dr. Dénes Szieberth, a university lecturer at BME, and students passionate about the underwater world. Since then, the club's main goal has been to popularize diving and raise interest in the aquatic world among students and employees of the Budapest University of Technology and Economics.

Members of the club include all age groups, from university students to graduated engineers and families. What we have in common is a passion for the underwater world and a desire for discovery. Our annual programs include domestic and international diving trips, where there is an opportunity to discover the most beautiful diving sites. Over the past years, we have dived in the Adriatic in Croatia, the Red Sea in Egypt, and many other exciting places. These trips not only provide diving opportunities but also unforgettable shared experiences. In addition, there are club evenings and lectures where we can share our experiences and insights with each other about various aspects of diving. Furthermore, club members participate in various underwater research projects. Our goal is to contribute to the protection of aquatic life and environmental protection.

Within the framework of the club, it is possible to complete various levels of diving courses, from beginner to advanced. Not a few of our members dive in caves, and many of them started diving at the BME Diving Club. The club's base is in Dorog, so we mostly go there, where we plant plants and tidy up the terrain so that we can see as much as possible underwater. During these times, we always have the opportunity to practice according to our level. But what distinguishes us from other clubs? Combining engineering and natural science knowledge with diving is the creation of the 'scientific diver' program at BME, already well-known at foreign universities. With practical and theoretical knowledge, we can safely and effectively carry out measurements, observations, and sampling underwater. The community of the BME Diving Club helps everyone with diving questions from the very beginning, for example, what is worth buying where, and with non-diving questions alike.
Furthermore, every spring semester we offer a freely elective subject for those interested in the mysteries of diving. The subject's name is: Diving from an engineering perspective (BMEVESAA010).

If you are interested in diving, want to be a member of a friendly and supportive community, or simply want to know more about the underwater world, then join us! We welcome everyone, whether a beginner or an experienced diver.
You can find all necessary information on our Facebook and Instagram pages, but feel free to contact us if you have any questions.
Dive with us, discover the depths yourself!

korok.title: BME Rueda Dance Club
description: The BME Rueda Dance Club was formed in the spring of 2021 with the aim of creating an opportunity for university students to get to know and master various social and individual dances, and to create a dancing community. We currently offer courses where students can get to know Cuban salsa, Dominican bachata, and Latin ballroom dances.

Our further goal is to offer sophisticated entertainment and networking opportunities where lifelong friendships or even romantic relationships can be born. That's why we regularly hold dance parties, summer camps, and outdoor parties!
Come and let's dance through the semester together!

korok.title: BME Tíztánc
description: BME Tíztánc is a university self-organizing club established in 2019. Our main goal is to popularize standard and Latin dance genres among students of the Budapest University of Technology and Economics (BME). Our team was created following the model of 'Ballroom dancing team' groups at MIT and other renowned universities, and it basically encompasses ten dances (5 Latin and 5 standard). The goal of our cohesive and friendly community is to establish competitive dance culture at BME, during which we wish to provide professional yet friendly competitions for those wishing to compete.

Our team treats attendance requirements flexibly; however, to achieve progress, it is recommended to participate in guided classes as often as possible. BME Tíztánc is addressed exclusively to active BME students, primarily in the 18-30 age group. We welcome all new interested people, whether beginners or old dancers, and no partner or prior dance experience is required to join.

Currently, teaching is conducted in three different groups: beginner, intermediate, and advanced levels. The beginner group is for those who have never encountered these dances before, while the intermediate and advanced groups are recommended mainly for more experienced dancers. We provide 2x90 minutes of guided classes per week for each group, held by our professional competitive dance instructors. In addition, we also provide practice opportunities for our members several times a week.

We organize numerous events and programs inside and outside the university, for example, a Gala, Mid-semester Ball, team-building events, and performances. Furthermore, we hold a dance camp in the summer, and we now participate in the ETDS international dance competition for university students every semester. Our events help us get to know each other better and weld ourselves into a true team. Everyone in our club can find challenges and dance opportunities suitable for their own level, and thanks to the friendly and supportive atmosphere, countless lifelong friendships have been formed among us over the years.

korok.title: Műegyetemi Fencing Club
description: The Műegyetemi Fencing Club started its operation in 2016; its primary goal is to provide regular training opportunities for fencers studying at BME. Since its formation, the team's membership and equipment have expanded significantly; there are athletes from all faculties of the university on our team, and all three weapon types of fencing (sabre, épée, foil) are available with us.

We place great emphasis on popularizing the sport; to this end, we organize open training sessions annually, where we welcome all enthusiastic people interested in getting to know fencing more closely, as well as - for the 3rd year now - we are successfully launching beginner courses where one can learn the beauties of the sport from the basics.

Our club is continuously looking for international and domestic opportunities to build relationships with as many similar university-age fencing communities as possible, as a result of which we had the opportunity to participate in an international competition in China, and the fencing team of BME's partner university (Karlsruhe Institute of Technology) hosted us for a training camp.

Competitions are also not left out of the team's life; every year we participate in the Hungarian University and College National Championship (MEFOB), where BME has been regularly winning the overall points competition for years. In addition, it is possible to join the Műegyetemi Athletic and Football Club (MAFC) Fencing Department, so one can also compete in the competitions of the Hungarian Fencing Federation in a university team, with fencers of the same age group.
If we have sparked your interest, you used to fence, or you would like to start in the future, feel free to contact us!

erasmus.title: Erasmus+

general_info.title: What is Erasmus+?
general_info.description: The Erasmus+ programme is one of BME's most popular international opportunities, allowing you to try yourself abroad – either in the form of a study semester or an internship. A new country, new culture, and plenty of experiences await, while you don't have to interrupt your studies at home. Erasmus+ is not just a scholarship, but a lifelong adventure that improves your language skills, gives you independence, and helps you build international connections – both professionally and personally.

eelisa.title: EELISA

what_is_it.title: What is EELISA?
what_is_it.description: EELISA (European Engineering Learning Innovation and Science Alliance) is an alliance of nine European universities, involving engineering, technical, and broader educational institutions from eight countries. The initiative aims to jointly rethink what it means to be a European engineer today – training professionals who contribute to shaping the future not only with their technical knowledge but also with their social responsibility and broad perspective. By connecting engineering, scientific, and humanities fields, EELISA is working to create a more inclusive, sustainable, and digital Europe.
